fullArticle:
Sign up now: Get ST's newsletters delivered to your inbox

The UBS logo is pictured on a building next to the Federal Palace of Switzerland, in Bern, Switzerland, December 11, 2025. REUTERS/Pierre Albouy

ZURICH, June 30 - Personal wealth in 2025 grew at its fastest pace in years, creating nearly one million new U.S. dollar millionaires worldwide, Swiss bank UBS said in its annual Global Wealth Report published on Tuesday.

• Total personal wealth globally rose by 10.8% last year, up from 4.6% in 2024 and 4.2% in 2023, as strong financial markets boosted growth, UBS found.

• There were "more millionaires than ever, everywhere" in 2025, the bank said. The United States, where over 440,000 people became new U.S. dollar millionaires, accounted for almost half of this growth.
